name: wpasupplicant
title: wpasupplicant
summary: client support for WPA and WPA2 (IEEE 802.11i)
description:
client support for WPA and WPA2 (IEEE 802.11i)
wpa-supplicant is a userspace daemon handling connection and authentication
in wireless and wired networks, primarily secured with the WPA/WPA2/WPA3
protocols. This software provides key negotiation with the access point
(WPA Authenticator), and controls association with IEEE 802.11i networks.

commands.name: wpa_passphrase
commands.description: Generate a WPA PSK from an ASCII passphrase for a SSID

arguments.description: The SSID whose passphrase should be derived.
arguments.raw: -bit  The SSID whose passphrase should be derived.

arguments.description: network configuration blocks of a wpa_suppli-
arguments.raw: -computes  network configuration blocks of a wpa_suppli-

commands.name: wpa_supplicant
commands.description: Wi-Fi Protected Access client and IEEE 802.1X supplicant

command_examples: wpa_passphrase  pre-computes  PSK  entries  for  network configuration blocks of a wpa_suppli-
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use wpa_passphrase because client support for WPA and WPA2 (IEEE 802.11i).
When the engagement is in the install phase, use wpa_passphrase as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `wpa_passphrase [ ssid ] [ passphrase ]` as documented upstream for wpa_passphrase.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `wpa_passphrase  pre-computes  PSK  entries  for  network configuration blocks of a wpa_suppli-` as documented upstream for wpa_passphrase.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair wpa_passphrase with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `wpa_passphrase --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

arguments.name: -bit
arguments.description: The SSID whose passphrase should be derived.

arguments.name: -computes
arguments.description: network configuration blocks of a wpa_suppli-
